  "text": "Node.js core defends against prototype pollution through several coordinated mechanisms, primarily in `github.com/nodejs/node`.\n\n## 1. The Primordials System (`lib/internal/per_context/primordials.js`)\nThis is the **central defense file**. It captures all JS built-in constructors and methods at startup before user code can mutate them. Key techniques:\n- **`uncurryThis`**: Binds `Function.prototype.call` before it can be tampered with, used to safely call prototype methods\n- **`makeSafe(unsafe, safe)`**: Creates subclasses of `Map`, `Set`, `WeakMap`, `WeakSet`, `FinalizationRegistry`, and `WeakRef` with their prototypes set to `null` and frozen — producing `SafeMap`, `SafeSet`, `SafeWeakMap`, `SafeWeakSet`, `SafeFinalizationRegistry`, `SafeWeakRef`\n- **`hardenRegExp(pattern)`**: Overrides all user-overridable RegExp methods with original primordial versions, preventing `[Symbol.match]` and friends from being abused\n- **`{ __proto__: null, ...desc }` pattern**: Used pervasively when defining property descriptors to ensure descriptor objects themselves have no prototype chain\n- **`ObjectSetPrototypeOf(primordials, null)`** and **`ObjectFreeze(primordials)`**: The entire primordials namespace has its own prototype removed and is frozen\n- Safe Promise combinators: `SafePromiseAll`, `SafePromiseAllSettled`, `SafePromiseAllReturnArrayLike`, `SafePromiseAllReturnVoid`, `SafePromiseAllSettledReturnVoid`, `SafePromiseAny`, `SafePromiseRace`, `SafePromisePrototypeFinally` — all wrap calls to avoid exposing SafePromise prototype to user-land\n\n## 2. `kEmptyObject` and `kEnumerableProperty` (`lib/internal/util.js`)\n- **`kEmptyObject = ObjectFreeze({ __proto__: null })`**: A reusable frozen empty object with no prototype, used as the safe default for options parameters throughout the codebase (prevents `options.constructor`, `options.__proto__`, etc. from causing pollution)\n- **`kEnumerableProperty = { __proto__: null }; kEnumerableProperty.enumerable = true`**: A safe property descriptor prototype\n- **`encodingsMap = { __proto__: null }`**: Encodings lookup map created without a prototype\n\n## 3. `ObjectPrototypeHasOwnProperty` / `ObjectHasOwn` checks\nThroughout `lib/`, the primordials alias `ObjectPrototypeHasOwnProperty` (equivalent to `Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(obj, key)`) is used instead of `obj.hasOwnProperty(key)` to guard `for...in` loops and object property access. Key files:\n- `lib/internal/freeze_intrinsics.js`: Guards property access when freezing all JS intrinsics\n- `lib/internal/modules/cjs/loader.js`: Guards module cache lookups; also uses `{ __proto__: null }` for `Module._cache`, `Module._pathCache`, `Module._extensions`\n- `lib/internal/modules/esm/assert.js`, `get_format.js`, `resolve.js`, `translators.js`, `helpers.js`, `typescript.js`: ESM loader uses `ObjectPrototypeHasOwnProperty` when checking import attributes and package exports\n- `lib/internal/http2/core.js` and `lib/internal/http2/compat.js`: HTTP/2 headers are stored in `Object.create(null)` objects (explicitly noted as intentional for security vs HTTP/1 which could not be changed); `ObjectHasOwn` guards header iteration\n- `lib/_http_outgoing.js`: Uses `ObjectHasOwn` when processing HTTP/1 response headers\n- `lib/internal/crypto/util.js`, `lib/internal/crypto/webidl.js`: Guards over registered algorithm maps\n- `lib/internal/source_map/source_map.js`, `lib/internal/process/promises.js`, `lib/internal/assert/assertion_error.js`, `lib/internal/encoding/single-byte.js`, `lib/internal/util/inspector.js`: Safe property checks in various utilities\n- `lib/internal/util/parse_args/utils.js` and `parse_args.js`: `objectGetOwn` and `optionsGetOwn` helpers built on `ObjectHasOwn`\n- `lib/internal/vm/module.js`, `lib/internal/tty.js`, `lib/cluster.js`, `lib/internal/cli_table.js`: Scattered use of `ObjectPrototypeHasOwnProperty` for safe lookups\n\n## 4. `Object.create(null)` / `{ __proto__: null }` for data containers\n- `lib/internal/modules/cjs/loader.js`: All Module caches use `{ __proto__: null }` to prevent `__proto__`, `constructor`, or `toString` keys from being treated as special\n- `lib/quic.js`: Constants objects use `ObjectCreate(null, { __proto__: null, ... })` combined with `ObjectSeal` for tamper-proof constant maps\n- `lib/internal/error_serdes.js`: `TryGetAllProperties` accumulates into `{ __proto__: null }` to avoid prototype chain interference\n- `lib/internal/bootstrap/realm.js`, `lib/internal/bootstrap/node.js`: Bootstrap binding objects use `{ __proto__: null }` to safely receive native bindings\n- `lib/internal/modules/esm/create_dynamic_module.js`: Module reflection objects created with `{ __proto__: null }`\n- `lib/internal/events/abort_listener.js`: Return objects use `{ __proto__: null }`\n- `lib/internal/heap_utils.js`, `lib/internal/webstreams/queuingstrategies.js`, `lib/internal/perf/nodetiming.js`, `lib/internal/streams/lazy_transform.js`, `lib/internal/webstorage.js`: Inspect option objects and property descriptors use `{ __proto__: null }`\n\n## 5. ESLint Enforcement (`tools/eslint-rules/prefer-primordials.js`)\nAn ESLint rule enforces that core modules use primordials instead of global builtins, preventing accidental use of mutable methods like `Object.create`, `hasOwnProperty`, `Array.prototype.forEach`, etc. directly.\n\n## 6. Safe Collection Usage\n`SafeMap` and `SafeSet` (defined in primordials.js) are used extensively in performance-critical core modules (`lib/internal/trace_events_async_hooks.js`, `lib/internal/watch_mode/files_watcher.js`, `lib/internal/bootstrap/realm.js`, `lib/internal/fs/recursive_watch.js`, `lib/internal/perf/usertiming.js`, `lib/internal/console/constructor.js`, `lib/internal/modules/esm/translators.js`, `lib/internal/cluster/child.js`, `lib/internal/fs/glob.js`, etc.) to ensure that iteration and storage operations cannot be poisoned by user code overriding `Map.prototype[Symbol.iterator]` or similar."
}

  "text": "Node.js core in github.com/nodejs/node defends against prototype pollution through several coordinated mechanisms.\n\n## 1. The Primordials System (lib/internal/per_context/primordials.js)\nThis is the **central defense file**. It captures all JS built-in constructors and methods at startup before user code can mutate them. Key techniques:\n- **`uncurryThis`**: Binds `Function.prototype.call` before it can be tampered with, used to safely call prototype methods without going through the prototype chain\n- **`applyBind`**: Same pattern using `Function.prototype.apply`\n- **`makeSafe(unsafe, safe)`**: Creates subclasses of `Map`, `Set`, `WeakMap`, `WeakSet`, `FinalizationRegistry`, and `WeakRef` with their prototypes set to `null` via `ObjectSetPrototypeOf(safe.prototype, null)` and frozen — producing `SafeMap`, `SafeSet`, `SafeWeakMap`, `SafeWeakSet`, `SafeFinalizationRegistry`, `SafeWeakRef`\n- **`createSafeIterator`**: Creates safe iterator classes whose prototypes are also set to null and frozen\n- **`hardenRegExp(pattern)`**: Overrides all user-overridable RegExp methods (`[Symbol.match]`, `[Symbol.matchAll]`, `[Symbol.replace]`, `[Symbol.search]`, `[Symbol.split]`, `exec`, `flags`, etc.) with the original primordial versions, preventing poisoned RegExp objects from hijacking string operations\n- **`{ __proto__: null, ...desc }` pattern**: Used pervasively when defining property descriptors to ensure descriptor objects themselves have no prototype chain\n- **`ObjectSetPrototypeOf(primordials, null)` and `ObjectFreeze(primordials)`**: The entire primordials namespace has its prototype removed and is frozen at the end of the file\n- **Safe Promise combinators**: `SafePromiseAll`, `SafePromiseAllSettled`, `SafePromiseAllReturnArrayLike`, `SafePromiseAllReturnVoid`, `SafePromiseAllSettledReturnVoid`, `SafePromiseAny`, `SafePromiseRace`, `SafePromisePrototypeFinally` — all wrap calls to avoid exposing `SafePromise` prototype to user-land\n- **`SafeStringPrototypeSearch`** and **`SafeArrayPrototypePushApply`**: Utility functions that use primordials versions of underlying methods\n\n## 2. kEmptyObject and kEnumerableProperty (lib/internal/util.js)\n- **`kEmptyObject = ObjectFreeze({ __proto__: null })`**: A reusable frozen empty The Primordials System (lib/internal/per_context/primordials.js)\nThis is the **central defense file**. It captures all JS built-in constructors and methods at startup before user code can mutate them. Key techniques:\n- **`uncurryThis`**: Binds `Function.prototype.call` before it can be tampered with, used to safely call prototype methods without going through the prototype chain\n- **`applyBind`**: Same pattern using `Function.prototype.apply`\n- **`makeSafe(unsafe, safe)`**: Creates subclasses of `Map`, `Set`, `WeakMap`, `WeakSet`, `FinalizationRegistry`, and `WeakRef` with their prototypes set to `null` via `ObjectSetPrototypeOf(safe.prototype, null)` and frozen — producing `SafeMap`, `SafeSet`, `SafeWeakMap`, `SafeWeakSet`, `SafeFinalizationRegistry`, `SafeWeakRef`\n- **`createSafeIterator`**: Creates safe iterator classes whose prototypes are also set to null and frozen\n- **`hardenRegExp(pattern)`**: Overrides all user-overridable RegExp methods (`[Symbol.match]`, `[Symbol.matchAll]`, `[Symbol.replace]`, `[Symbol.search]`, `[Symbol.split]`, `exec`, `flags`, etc.) with the original primordial versions, preventing poisoned RegExp objects from hijacking string operations\n- **`{ __proto__: null, ...desc }` pattern**: Used pervasively when defining property descriptors to ensure descriptor objects themselves have no prototype chain\n- **`ObjectSetPrototypeOf(primordials, null)` and `ObjectFreeze(primordials)`**: The entire primordials namespace has its prototype removed and is frozen at the end of the file\n- **Safe Promise combinators**: `SafePromiseAll`, `SafePromiseAllSettled`, `SafePromiseAllReturnArrayLike`, `SafePromiseAllReturnVoid`, `SafePromiseAllSettledReturnVoid`, `SafePromiseAny`, `SafePromiseRace`, `SafePromisePrototypeFinally` — all wrap calls to avoid exposing `SafePromise` prototype to user-land\n- **`SafeStringPrototypeSearch`** and **`SafeArrayPrototypePushApply`**: Utility functions that use primordials versions of underlying methods\n\n## 2. kEmptyObject and kEnumerableProperty (lib/internal/util.js)\n- **`kEmptyObject = ObjectFreeze({ __proto__: null })`**: A reusable frozen empty object with no prototype, used as the safe default for options parameters throughout the codebase. Prevents `options.constructor`, `options.__proto__`, or `options.toString` from causing pollution when options are spread or iterated.\n- **`kEnumerableProperty = { __proto__: null }; kEnumerableProperty.enumerable = true`**: A safe property descriptor object with no prototype\n- **`encodingsMap = { __proto__: null }`**: Encoding lookup map without prototype\n\n## 3. ObjectPrototypeHasOwnProperty / ObjectHasOwn checks\nThroughout lib/, the primordials alias `ObjectPrototypeHasOwnProperty` (equivalent to `Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(obj, key)`) and `ObjectHasOwn` are used instead of `obj.hasOwnProperty(key)` to guard `for...in` loops and object property access:\n- **lib/internal/freeze_intrinsics.js**: Guards property access when freezing all JS intrinsics to prevent __proto__ / constructor keys from interfering\n- **lib/internal/modules/cjs/loader.js**: Guards module cache lookups; `Module._cache`, `Module._pathCache`, `Module._extensions` all use `{ __proto__: null }` to prevent key collisions with Object prototype\n- **lib/internal/modules/esm/assert.js, get_format.js, resolve.js, translators.js, helpers.js, typescript.js**: ESM loader uses `ObjectPrototypeHasOwnProperty` when checking import attributes, protocol handlers, and package exports\n- **lib/internal/http2/core.js and lib/internal/http2/compat.js**: HTTP/2 headers stored in `Object.create(null)` objects (explicitly noted as intentional for security vs HTTP/1); `ObjectHasOwn` guards header iteration\n- **lib/_http_outgoing.js**: Uses `ObjectHasOwn` when iterating user-supplied HTTP/1 response headers\n- **lib/internal/crypto/util.js, lib/internal/crypto/webidl.js**: Guards registered algorithm maps; uses `ObjectPrototypeIsPrototypeOf` for interface checks\n- **lib/internal/source_map/source_map.js, lib/internal/process/promises.js, lib/internal/assert/assertion_error.js, lib/internal/encoding/single-byte.js, lib/internal/util/inspector.js**: Scattered safe property checks in utilities\n- **lib/internal/util/parse_args/utils.js and parse_args.js**: `objectGetOwn` and `optionsGetOwn` helpers built on `ObjectHasOwn`\n- **lib/internal/vm/module.js, lib/internal/tty.js, lib/cluster.js, lib/internal/cli_table.js**: Safe `for...in` iteration using `ObjectPrototypeHasOwnProperty`\n\n## 4. Object.create(null) / { __proto__: null } for Data Containers\n- **lib/internal/modules/cjs/loader.js**: All Module caches use `{ __proto__: null }` to prevent `__proto__`, `constructor`, or `toString` keys from being treated as special\n- **lib/quic.js**: Constants objects use `ObjectCreate(null, { __proto__: null, ... })` combined with `ObjectSeal` for tamper-proof constant maps\n- **lib/internal/error_serdes.js**: `TryGetAllProperties` accumulates into `{ __proto__: null }` to avoid prototype chain interference\n- **lib/internal/bootstrap/realm.js, lib/internal/bootstrap/node.js**: Bootstrap binding objects use `{ __proto__: null }` to safely receive native bindings\n- **lib/internal/modules/esm/create_dynamic_module.js**: Module reflection objects (`exports`, `imports`) created with `{ __proto__: null }`\n- **lib/internal/events/abort_listener.js**: Return objects use `{ __proto__: null }`\n- **lib/internal/heap_utils.js, lib/internal/webstreams/queuingstrategies.js, lib/internal/perf/nodetiming.js, lib/internal/streams/lazy_transform.js, lib/internal/webstorage.js**: Inspect option objects and property descriptors use `{ __proto__: null }`\n- **lib/internal/errors.js**: SystemError property definitions use `{ __proto__: null }` for descriptors\n- **lib/internal/test_runner/reporter/**: Test reporter objects (junit.js, rerun.js, utils.js) and tests_stream.js emit event data objects with `{ __proto__: null }`\n\n## 5. Safe Collection Usage\n`SafeMap` and `SafeSet` are used extensively in core modules (`lib/internal/trace_events_async_hooks.js`, `lib/internal/watch_mode/files_watcher.js`, `lib/internal/bootstrap/realm.js`, `lib/internal/fs/recursive_watch.js`, `lib/internal/perf/usertiming.js`, `lib/internal/console/constructor.js`, `lib/internal/modules/esm/translators.js`, `lib/internal/cluster/child.js`, `lib/internal/fs/glob.js`, etc.) to ensure that iteration and storage operations cannot be poisoned by user code overriding `Map.prototype[Symbol.iterator]` or `Set.prototype[Symbol.iterator]`."
